name: Mike Thompson
subject: Congratulations to Joe Biden and Kamala Harris
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Congratulations to Joe Biden and Kamala Harris as they are sworn in as President and Vice President of the United States.

We worked hard to elect a democratic President — and our hard work has officially come to fruition.

It’s because of your time and dedication that today we call Joe Biden our 46th President of the United States. It’s because of you that Kamala Harris will be the first female Vice President of the United States. It’s because of you that our nation is taking a giant leap toward a progressive future.

We’re at a turning point in American history — and I can’t thank you enough for fighting for our future and never giving up.

Thank you for making history!

Mike Thompson

name: Roger Misso
subject: We restored decency and honor to the White House
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Taking The Hill PAC
body:
Peter,

For four years, we worked hard for this day. We gave our time, treasure, and effort for this day. We endured sleepless nights and uncertainty about the future for this day.

On this day, it pays off. Joe Biden and Kamala Harris have been sworn in. Honor and decency have been restored to the White House.

America is back.

My hope is that we never take our democracy - any seat, anywhere - for granted ever again. Today, we are celebrating. Tomorrow, we will be back at work at Taking the Hill to elect veterans and military family members of integrity to Congress.

If we learned anything these last four years, let it be this: the work of defending our democracy endures. It takes all of us. And it is in striving together in our great, sovereign journey that we find our greatest reward: America.

With honor,
Roger

Roger Misso
Executive Director

name: Chairman Andrew Hitt
subject: Election Reform Initiative Launch
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF WISCONSIN
body:
Dear Friend,

As we enter a new year and new election cycle, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is committed to laying the groundwork for success in 2022 and 2024. We are expanding our field programs and are dedicated as always to growing and improving the best grassroots network in the country.

We must work to reform the election process so that all voters receive the honest and transparent elections to which they are entitled. In fact, in just this past year, we have taken two cases to the United States Supreme Court, and two more to the Wisconsin Supreme Court in our relentless efforts to promote election integrity. Throughout this effort, we've worked closely with President Trump's campaign.

Moving forward, the Republican Party of Wisconsin has formed an Election Reform Taskforce. This taskforce has county party leaders and grassroots activists from across the state as we fight for ballot integrity, find solutions to the problems and concerns that exist, and propose solutions to the Wisconsin State legislature.

As we enter a new year, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is committed to meeting our goals for growth and improvement, and building our Party from the grassroots level up. The only way we can achieve this is by continuously working to build a stronger foundation for our candidates up and down the ballot and with support from grassroots activists like you. From all of us at the Republican Party of Wisconsin, thank you for all you do for Republican and conservative causes, and please do not hesitate to reach out.

Best wishes,

Andrew Hitt, Chairman

clean_body: Dear Friend, As we enter a new year and new election cycle, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is committed to laying the groundwork for success in 2022 and 2024. We are expanding our field programs and are dedicated as always to growing and improving the best grassroots network in the country. We must work to reform the election process so that all voters receive the honest and transparent elections to which they are entitled. In fact, in just this past year, we have taken two cases to the United States Supreme Court, and two more to the Wisconsin Supreme Court in our relentless efforts to promote election integrity. Throughout this effort, we've worked closely with President Trump's campaign. Moving forward, the Republican Party of Wisconsin has formed an Election Reform Taskforce. This taskforce has county party leaders and grassroots activists from across the state as we fight for ballot integrity, find solutions to the problems and concerns that exist, and propose solutions to the Wisconsin State legislature. As we enter a new year, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is committed to meeting our goals for growth and improvement, and building our Party from the grassroots level up. The only way we can achieve this is by continuously working to build a stronger foundation for our candidates up and down the ballot and with support from grassroots activists like you. From all of us at the Republican Party of Wisconsin, thank you for all you do for Republican and conservative causes, and please do not hesitate to reach out. Best wishes, Andrew Hitt, Chairman This message was intended for: mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com You were added to the system May 22, 2018. name: Greg Stanton
subject: A new day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
body:
Friend,

America is a place where anything is possible. Though the last two weeks showed us that even the unthinkable can happen, today’s triumph of democracy is proof that it's ultimately up to us to define those possibilities.

Today’s inauguration of a new president is a reminder not only of the possibilities ahead of us – but also of our responsibilities.

Each of us has a role to play in making our Union more perfect – and it’s a task that demands something from all of us.

I’m ready to do my part – to work with you, my colleagues in Congress, and with President Joe Biden to improve the lives of every Arizonan: to make our economy work for every family, modernize our immigration laws and pass the Dream Act, and end this tragic pandemic once and for all.

I hope you are too.

Let’s get to work.

--Greg

Now, I have been called to serve the people again. I am running for Congress in district 23. Please contribute to my campaign now.

It is in my blood, I want to help the citizens of Texas l | | | | | | | | The Time is Now | | As many of you know, I have a passion for helping and serving the people of Texas. It is in my blood, I want to help the citizens of Texas live life to the fullest and achieve their vision of the American Dream. As a small business woman, nurse, and former gubernatorial appointee and state commissioner of the Texas Holocaust & Genocide Commission and an advisory council cabinet member of Governor Rick Perry and Greg Abbott, I have had the privledge to help my fellow Texans. Now, I have been called to serve the people again. I am running for Congress in district 23. Please contribute to my campaign now. | | | | | My opponent who has been in office for two weeks has already failed the citizens of the 23rd district by not standing up for election integrity. He did not vote against the electoral certification. We know that voter fraud was committed in the presidential race. My opponent should have advocated for open and honest elections for the registered voters in the 23rd district but he did not. If he failed at something so vital to our republic and election process, what other issues will he take the easy way out and ignore? I am asking you to consider contributing what you can today, so we may have a representative that is true to our conservative American values and is not afraid to stand up for honest and lawful elections for the people of Texas! name: Melissa Hortman
subject: Let’s build back better
disclaimer_text: paid for by the DFL House Caucus
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/zOYJyfS9hXHLKy8oryQF7hpTIiIZQYuzYO7qaeOdLrk=/17DQBhACI8IwJgzpYaQHt-uCSyoLeqeS0CqtzqwcymQ=?refcode=012021)
| | Friend, Today marks the beginning of a new era for our country. From the moment Lady Gaga started singing the national anthem, I could not hold back tears. I think we can all breathe a sigh of relief today knowing President Biden is in the White House. On top of it all, Vice President Harris shattered a major glass ceiling today, and it was long overdue. President Biden's call for unity this morning was a powerful message and one we all need to hear. Our country and our state face very significant challenges in the months and days ahead. The COVID-19 pandemic has ripped through our country, taking over 400,000 mothers, fathers, children, friends, and family from us. One thing I know is that together, we will defeat this virus and build back better, with a focus on equity and justice for all. If the vision for our country and state laid out by President Biden today is one you believe in as well, please support us in the work ahead by making a refundable $50 (or $100 per couple) contribution through the Minnesota Political Contribution Refund Program. Happy Inauguration Day. Thank you, **Melissa Hortman** Speaker Minnesota House of Representatives [**Donate**](https://act.myngp.com/el/zOYJyfS9hXHLKy8oryQF7hpTIiIZQYuzYO7qaeOdLrk=/17DQBhACI8IwJgzpYaQHt-uCSyoLeqeS0CqtzqwcymQ=?refcode=012021)

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: A day for celebration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elissa Slotkin for Congress
body:
Hi team,

Today was a momentous, historic day. Dave and I were in attendance at the inauguration, and I felt such pride watching President Biden and Vice President Harris get sworn in.

After we got through security, we headed to our assigned seats on the Capitol lawn. It was a cold, but sunny and crisp day here in DC, and Dave and I had a great view from our seats.

Watching Vice President Harris be sworn in made me think of my own young nieces, who will get to grow up in a world where they see women in the highest ranks of government. Representation matters, and it is a powerful thing to have an African-American and South Asian American woman be sworn in as Vice President of the United States.

President Biden’s speech really resonated with me. As I have been saying for months, we have to find ways to bring the temperature down in our politics, and unify. That isn’t going to be easy. But we must all work to build back the connective tissue that binds our communities together. As President Biden said, “Let’s begin to listen to one another again, hear one another, see one another, show respect to one another.” It’s a message I’ll continue to take with me, as we begin the important work of coming together.

As we walked over to the Capitol this morning, Dave and I reflected back on the last inauguration. Four years ago, we were canoeing on the Rio Grande, without any thought of me ever running for Congress. Much has changed over the past four years. But today, I feel a profound sense of hope and optimism. Now, we begin the important work of unifying our country, and moving forward as the United States of America.

Let’s get to work,

Elissa

Elissa

name: Jay Inslee
subject: A future built on science, empathy, and respect
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
Why I'm celebrating today.
| [](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/8912?t=3&akid=8236%2E868730%2EGHUQps) Friend, For the last four years, we've been fighting for real action and partnership from our national leadership -- and starting today, we're finally going to get it. With the inauguration of Joe Biden as our next president, and Kamala Harris as our next vice president, we'll start the process we've so desperately fought for to save our planet and our people. We've seen here in Washington state what happens if we embrace sound, scientific, and responsible actions to protect our planet and each other. We've taken aggressive steps in our climate policy and have plans for further progress. We know that fighting climate change is also an opportunity to support good-paying jobs, make our infrastructure cleaner and more resilient, and invest in our communities to ensure a more equitable future for our kids and grandkids. Following science is also how we've made progress against COVID-19. Early on, Washington state was hit hard by the pandemic, but since then, we've worked hard, followed the science, and saved lives, keeping folks safe and healthy. Reporting has shown that if the entire nation had followed our example, many thousands of American lives would have been saved -- and that's entirely due to our commitment to base our decisions on science and empathy. This is the type of leadership we will get from President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris: an embrace of science and an agenda based on empathy and respect. And we'll finally have a partner in Washington, D.C. committed to progress on climate, economic equity, racial justice, COVID, and more. [**I hope you'll join me in wishing them the best of luck as they head to the White House today -- send a congratulations tweet with me in support of this new chapter for our country, and commit to supporting their work to build back better.**](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/14100?t=4&akid=8236%2E868730%2EGHUQps) Very truly yours, Jay | [TWEET NOW](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/14100?t=5&akid=8236%2E868730%2EGHUQps)

| Hoosier Republican,

It’s been an incredible experience to watch our former Governor, Mike Pence, excel in the role of Vice President alongside President Trump.

The Vice President played a key role in passing historic tax cuts in 2017. He chaired the White House Coronavirus Task Force which delivered COVID vaccines quicker than anyone imagined, and he’s stood tough against China on the international stage to strengthen America’s standing in the global economy.

Mike Pence has made Indiana proud while serving on the global stage and now that his term as Vice President has concluded, [_**we want YOU to sign his Thank You card as he returns to Indiana**_](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e4b06c363d&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Sending him your thanks and well wishes is easy! [_**All you need to do is click or tap here to sign his e-card**_](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c41e043096&e=1acd9bb3d6). Don’t miss your chance though! We’ll be giving him the list of supporters the moment he steps back on Hoosier soil.

Thanks for your support,

Your Republican Team

It’s official –– now that President Joe Biden has been inaugurated, Democrats are in control of the White House, the Senate, and Congress –– and they’re not going to waste any time working towards checking off their agenda.

**That means strong, conservative leadership is now more important than ever before.**

Over the last four years, we worked tirelessly to create groundbreaking conservative victories that continue to deliver real results for Americans from coast to coast.

_We passed historic tax cuts, secured our borders, and strengthened our economy with trade deals that put American companies and workers first._

**But those victories are already in danger of being dismantled by the Left, and we’ll have to work even harder at defending them over the next four years.**

That starts right here, right now, on day one.
[](http://links.lahoodforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d5887efe66684ada97976ef2e9d70137&_m=ec142ad0c2b04af9b84e994190534a44&_e=l83poxGMuYPC3NsIvWZHMT4vqrDKWD2E_-zPBxOVlKRsrgs9tNlzcuYCg97ZfzZ2-ItEJ5-mdiBQsyPjgMNk_VJbrs47aJtIeL9cuMbXdh85vDJA9-MWVocCwKviticqpmXOIVGX_HND0023foca4DaGpy0hwNOmpQfFGIoVF_K7m2GrH8GSzTNQJ9JxJmShBJRad_GbRmXKJddwaEeAxI5zM_bPYgf_QzuU5AMMRUllsf0GgrR-PZaXJvRcyZUe3XopYL3z77O_XzJKEMRbMpyRtuKLrQ5iVFriSfj8oLXySZFVL2uaRMoDNFUdWulTGn5v_psoXmkRYUPCFlFH5FlVr7ckvodP77oOlnQcVEpXPkOgYobLbEaGjIue8dZQxDspUCCgrjcsiobgInPR8pbHQuU0vI7PF31ie1hpzmV5NR282KpevaIcxCFnrchGqE_1c_Va37G1-FToXS-fs8o-8cL7fG-eMWb7RNCVdhAXq1JIf_bHW9q0gcm4Duen)
From Peoria to Washington, our team is going to be standing up at every turn to protect our Constitutional rights and conservative values.

But we’re not going to be able to do it alone.

**We’re going to need all the support we can get at the grassroots level from patriots like you, Friend ––[will you start by simply adding your name today on day one of the Biden Administration?](http://links.lahoodforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d5887efe66684ada97976ef2e9d70137&_m=ec142ad0c2b04af9b84e994190534a44&_e=l83poxGMuYPC3NsIvWZHMT4vqrDKWD2E_-zPBxOVlKRsrgs9tNlzcuYCg97ZfzZ2-ItEJ5-mdiBQsyPjgMNk_VJbrs47aJtIeL9cuMbXdh85vDJA9-MWVocCwKviticqpmXOIVGX_HND0023foca4DaGpy0hwNOmpQfFGIoVF_K7m2GrH8GSzTNQJ9JxJmShBJRad_GbRmXKJddwaEeAxI5zM_bPYgf_QzuU5AMMRUllsf0GgrR-PZaXJvRcyZUe3XopYL3z77O_XzJKEMRbMpyRtuKLrQ5iVFriSfj8oLWkOwZHnFGQhijLzozicOiPMj5WUnyCQRRRsRfzvh_3UT7TNkrl504EkdbYbIVN--JQHdSl9IWIJhEGQAkCpuiJhnpDAU4UWMv0vkCkivJRTua2Cp5DYz8oXzoKnBqWwUms2xbzzVKfShJDpyPs_IlJIH1K38Fxc712q4XT7aYiC8qKf8Rz_NLVMqw_0fD-HitgdzMzIZSfaZRfvunRDwZq)**
